description: Top-level DAG code does heavy I/O at parse time, slowing the scheduler for every DAG.
body: Failure mode: a DAG file performs expensive work at module top level (querying a database or API to build tasks) on every parse, so the scheduler's repeated parsing becomes slow and all DAGs' scheduling latency degrades, not just the offending one. Detection signals: rising DAG parse times and scheduler heartbeat lag, with the slow parse traced to top-level I/O in one DAG file. Do not scale the scheduler to brute-force through it. Safer fallback: move heavy work out of parse time into task execution, generate dynamic tasks from a stable cached/versioned input, keep top-level code lightweight, and confirm parse time returns within the scheduler budget.
